concerns.text: FR-002 mandates handling missing environmental values by 'flagging or imputing with documented defaults'. The tasks (T014, T015b) and plan explicitly mandate EXCLUSION of such records. No task exists to implement the 'imputation' or 'flagging for manual review' logic required by FR-002. The spec requirement is unimplemented.

FR-004 mandates 'data augmentation via bond rotation and atom masking'. The tasks (T022) and plan explicitly FORBID these methods, replacing them with 'functional-group-preserving edge dropout'. No task exists to implement the bond rotation/atom masking logic required by FR-004. The spec requirement is unimplemented.

SC-004 requires a 'power analysis' to be triggered if dataset <150. Task T015b only generates a 'power_analysis_warning.txt' file. A simple text warning does not constitute a statistical power analysis (calculating effect size, power, etc.). No task exists to perform the actual power analysis calculation.

US-2 Scenario 3 requires that 'augmentation via bond rotation and atom masking' improves or stabilizes validation scores. Since the tasks explicitly forbid these augmentation methods (T022), there is no task to implement the experiment required to satisfy this acceptance scenario.

Task T015b is tagged with dependency '(Depends on T016)', but T016 (Save raw/processed datasets) logically depends on the completion of T015b (Power analysis logic) to determine if the dataset is sufficient. The dependency direction appears reversed or circular, potentially blocking execution.
